title: digitaria marginata var. linkii
intro: Digitaria marginata var. linkii, also known as the Link's finger grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used in various herbal medicines due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Link's finger grass include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is often used in traditional folk medicine to treat various ailments.
